jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Experience with data modeling, warehousing and building ETL pipelines
- 3+ years of data engineering experience
- Bachelor's degree
jobs.description:
The North American Stores Expansion Team is seeking an experienced and motivated Data Engineer to work on driving strategic projects across multiple geographies. We collaborate with Retail, SCOT (Supply Chain Optimization Technologies), AIT (Amazon Inbound Transportation), Vendor Negotiations, and Canada Product teams to develop scalable analytics applications, AI/ML products, and research models that optimize operations and enable business expansion. The environment is fast-paced with constant new challenges to solve.

The ideal candidate will have good leadership skills, proven ability to develop, enhance, automate, and manage analytics models and data pipelines. The candidate will have strong data mining and modeling skills and will be comfortable facilitating idea creation and working from concept through to execution. This role will also build tools and support structures needed to analyze data and dive deep to determine root causes of systems errors. The candidate will also have the opportunity to present findings to cross functional team partners to drive improvements. The ideal candidate will have a proven ability to work with Applied Scientists, Data Scientists, Software Development Engineers, Product Managers and Business Partners to solve challenging analytical problems. The candidate must be comfortable using intellect, curiosity and technical ability to develop innovative solutions to business problems. The position requires ability to learn different aspects of the business and understand how to apply advanced analytics to solve high impact problems.

A qualified candidate must have demonstrated ability to manage medium and large scale automation and modeling projects, identify requirements and build methodology and tools that are mathematically grounded but also explainable operationally, apply technical skills allowing the models to adapt to changing attributes. Strong written and verbal communication skills are a must as this role will be in frequent contact with leadership. The person in this role will be expected to provide clear and concise explanation to results and approaches as well as provide opinion and guidance on problem solving.

Key job responsibilities
Design, develop and maintain scalable data models, ETL/ELT processes, and ML pipelines using AWS services (S3, Redshift, Glue, Athena, Lake Formation)

Analyze business data to optimize key processes in Retail and Supply Chain domains

Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Scientists, Engineers, Program Managers) to deliver data solutions and implement predictive models

Implement and maintain CI/CD pipelines while ensuring data governance, security, and compliance standards

Monitor, optimize, and enhance data infrastructure to support analytics and machine learning

Stay current with emerging technologies and mentor junior engineers in data engineering and MLOps best practices

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 2+ years of professional non-internship marketing experience
- Experience using data and metrics to measure impact and determine improvements
- Experience collaborating with cross-functional teams
jobs.description:
Prime Video brings together the world’s best creators with the most passionate communities in a way that no other entertainment brand can. We offer customers distinctive, critically acclaimed and award-winning original programming including TV series such as The Boys, Fallout and The Summer I Turned Pretty, movies like The Pickup, The Idea of You, and Road House, and exclusive Live Sports such as Thursday Night Football and NBA on Prime.

Prime Video offers a multi-dimensional experience that allows subscribers to fully immerse themselves in their fandom: to read the book, to listen to the soundtrack, to buy the costume, to replay live sports in real time, and more. The breadth and connection to the Amazon family of brands, along with the support of a passionate and diverse global team, makes Prime Video a company where creators of all kinds can build their future.

We are seeking a talented Associate Media Manager to support our integrated media team in planning, executing, and optimizing media strategies for Prime Video marketing campaigns. This role focuses on media execution, insights collection, and cross-functional collaboration to build a transparent view of campaign performance and drive data-informed decisions.

You will work closely with marketing, analysts, and media partners to ensure campaigns align with business goals and deliver measurable impact. This position blends strategic support and operational execution, developing and implementing go-to-market strategies across owned and operated digital platforms and paid media campaigns.

Key job responsibilities
Performance Monitoring & Insights – Collect, analyze, and socialize campaign performance data to inform strategic decisions. Generate regular performance reports for stakeholders, highlighting key metrics, trends, and improvement opportunities. Partner with cross-functional teams to identify actionable insights and recommend optimizations that enhance campaign effectiveness and drive measurable results.

Media Planning & Execution Support – Assist in developing data-driven media strategies for Prime Video campaigns, translating audience insights into actionable plans. Support media operations, timeline management, and performance forecasting to ensure campaigns are executed efficiently and deliver measurable impact.

Process Improvement & Standardization – Improve SOPs, enhance tool adoption, and perform governance audits to streamline media operations, including reporting dashboards and asset repositories. Identify and implement workflow efficiencies to improve campaign speed and accuracy across all media execution processes.


About the team
The integrated media team leads marketing campaigns across owned and operated digital platforms and paid media campaigns. They deliver high-impact, scaled launches for Amazon Original content, Live Events, and Sports on Prime Video.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of developing, negotiating and executing business agreements experience
- 5+ years of Go-To-Market, Business Development, Sales, or Consulting experience
- 5+ years of working with Data & AI related technologies, including, but not limited to, AI/ML, GenAI, Analytics, Database, and/or Storage experience
- Experience developing strategies that influence leadership decisions at the organizational level
- Experience working and communicating with multiple stakeholders and cross functional teams including direct and channel marketing, solution architect teams, product management and account management teams
- Experience working with stakeholders to plan and execute programs that are strategic in nature
- Experience in external enterprise customer-facing role as a technical lead, with strong oral and written communication skills, presenting to both large and small audiences
- Experience in strategic planning
jobs.description:
Role Description

The Partner GTM organization within AWS is seeking a Partner Programs Strategy Lead to architect, scale, and orchestrate the end-to-end partner journey for Data & AI across AWS. This role is central to designing the strategic frameworks, programs, and resources that enable partner success throughout their Data & AI journey with AWS. You will be responsible for identifying gaps in the current partner experience, securing cross-organizational investments, and building scalable mechanisms that drive awareness and adoption of the partner journey through both direct partner engagement and field enablement.

This is a highly strategic role requiring exceptional program design capabilities, stakeholder management skills, and the ability to orchestrate complex initiatives across multiple AWS organizations including Partner Core, field teams, and product groups. You'll serve as the architect of the partner journey, ensuring we have the right programs and resources in place—whether self-managed by our team or delivered through partnerships with other AWS initiatives—to support our preferred partners' success in Data & AI.

A critical component of this role is creating and executing a comprehensive scaling and adoption plan that reaches partners through multiple channels: direct partner engagement, PDM (Partner Development Manager) and PDS (Partner Development Specialist) enablement, regional team coordination, and innovative GenAI tools and interfaces that streamline partner access to journey resources. Additionally, you will be the external face of AWS Data & AI partner programs, serving as a thought leader who continuously refines our approach based on market research, customer insights, and partner feedback.



Key job responsibilities
Partner Journey Strategy & Continuous Innovation

Own and continuously evolve the end-to-end Data & AI partner journey framework, building on the existing foundation to ensure it remains relevant and competitive
Conduct systematic gap analysis of current partner programs and experiences to identify improvement opportunities
Synthesize partner feedback, market research, and customer insights to inform journey evolution and influence team priorities and strategies
Maintain a big-picture view on health of partner investments and support across the journey, identifying where additional resources or program changes are needed
Create measurement frameworks to track partner progression and program effectiveness
Thought Leadership & External Brand

Serve as the external face and voice of AWS Data & AI partner programs at industry events, partner conferences, and executive forums
Collaborate with market research teams to ensure partner journey design reflects current customer needs, partner capabilities, and market dynamics
Publish thought leadership content that articulates the value of the Data & AI partner journey and showcases partner success stories
Build AWS's reputation as a thought leader in Data & AI partner ecosystem development
Program Orchestration & Investment Securing

Identify and secure the right mix of self-managed programs and cross-AWS initiatives to support the partner journey
Develop compelling business cases to secure budget allocations and organizational capacity commitments for priority partner programs
Build strong partnerships with AWS Partner Core, field organizations, and product teams to leverage existing programs and resources
Design governance models and operating mechanisms to ensure cohesive program delivery across multiple teams
Awareness & Adoption Scaling

Build scalable mechanisms to drive awareness of the Data & AI partner journey directly to partners and through PDM/PDS enablement
Create standardized messaging, playbooks, and toolkits that can be leveraged across field teams
Establish feedback loops with partners and field teams to continuously improve the journey experience
Design and implement innovative GenAI tools and interfaces that streamline partner access to journey resources and enable self-service adoption
Coordinate with regional teams to ensure localized execution of the scaling and adoption plan
Strategic Planning & Team Coordination

Support yearly operational and quarterly planning cycles by providing strategic input on partner journey priorities and resource needs
Facilitate team coordination activities to ensure alignment on partner programs strategy
Establish reporting mechanisms to track progress against partner journey metrics and team objectives

About the team
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.
Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.
Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.
Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.
Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

jobs.title: Sr. Worldwide Partner Specialist - Database & Analytics, Data & AI GTM

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of non-internship professional software development experience
- 5+ years of programming with at least one software programming language experience
- 5+ years of leading design or architecture (design patterns, reliability and scaling) of new and existing systems experience
- Experience as a mentor, tech lead or leading an engineering team
jobs.description:
Do you want to join a team of talented software engineers who are helping to revolutionize mobility to the cloud for their customers? AWS is seeking an exceptional developer to work on the challenging problem of accelerating our customer's journey from on-premises computing to running their businesses on AWS.

As a software developer, you will be part of a team that designs and builds an agentic solution that helps large enterprise companies migrate their computing to AWS. Delivering a reliable and high-performance service at AWS scale requires engineers with extraordinary technical expertise and boundless creativity. At Amazon, we also expect engineers like you to interact with customers, to understand our business goals, and to innovate rapidly, delivering high-quality and ground-breaking functionality.

What does it take to thrive as a developer at AWS? You need to be creative, curious, responsible, and able to work with others to rapidly turn an idea into working code. You're excited about writing code, implementing big ideas, and helping to coach those around you to raise the team's bar for code quality. You want the opportunity to grow your technical and professional skills while helping AWS grow. You relish the opportunity to dig into complex operational issues and to help customers build the next generation of web applications. You're someone who knows how to work hard and deliver results but is also able to have fun while they are doing it.

Agentic AI drives innovation at the forefront of artificial intelligence, enabling customers to transform their businesses through cutting-edge AI solutions. We build and deliver the foundational AI services that power the future of cloud computing, helping organizations harness the potential of AI to solve their most complex challenges. Join our dynamic team of AI/ML practitioners, applied scientists, software engineers, and solution architects who work backwards from customer needs to create groundbreaking technologies. If you're passionate about shaping the future of AI while making a meaningful impact for customers worldwide, we want to hear from you.

About the team
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture

AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

- Bachelor's degree
jobs.description:
The Creator team's mission is to make Amazon the Earth's most desired destination for commerce creators and their content. We own the Associates and Influencer programs and brands across Amazon to ensure a cohesive experience, expand creators' opportunities to earn through innovation, and launch experiences that reinforce feelings of achievement for creators. Within Creators, our product team strives to make Amazon the best place for creators to earn. We collectively accomplish that mission by building best-in-class software for incentives and payments, insights and encouragement, storefronts and content, as well as foundational tech, including linking tools and user interfaces.

We are seeking a results-driven leader to architect the future of creator brand partnerships at Amazon through a revolutionary suite of products. This highly visible position will shape how creators and brands collaborate on our product, requiring a candidate who can both "Dive Deep" into creator needs and "Think Big" about the future of digital commerce. You will develop ambitious strategies that transform how creators monetize their influence, working closely with Engineering to build innovative solutions ranging from API-first systems to intuitive user experiences. This leader will pioneer new ways for creators to partner with brands across Amazon, working across functional domains, geographies, business lines, and Amazon companies to drive unprecedented creator success. Successful candidates are leaders who can prioritize well, are technically excellent, communicate clearly, and have a consistent track record of innovating and delivering results. You should have strong customer obsession with the ability to dive deep, strong data/analytical skills, a self-starter spirit, and experience convincing senior leadership to invest in transformative ideas.

Key job responsibilities
- Monitor industry changes, technologies, and trends, determining when they should or shouldn't drive strategic, tactical, or roadmap shifts.
- Lead for a suite of products and systems, determining which creator-obsessed features and net new ideas to prioritize, build, and how/when/where to launch.
- Constantly experiment, developing data-backed solutions for problems on behalf of customers and creators. Work in close partnership with our science team to test and learn iteratively. You will be responsible for setting experimentation priorities, guardrails, success metrics, and gaining approvals to execute innovative tests.
- Own performance metrics and success criteria for your products during business-as-usual times as well as during critical high-velocity events, key levers for success of the channel.
- Work with WW business development leads to navigate existing business logic and feature sets. Define requirements for new products and features leading Amazon to innovate in how we work with creators and help them reach current and future customers.
- Create sustainable cross-functional and cross-product mechanisms to drive expansion and innovation while monitoring product and business health.
- Reduce defects and improve business performance through innovation and automation. Tirelessly improve products that will benefit creators and Amazon while owning the tools automating compensation changes.
- Conduct complex business analyses that identify opportunities to drive growth in our general creator population by extending learnings from BD teams within and across countries, in addition to deep dives.
- Develop and maintain relationships with key stakeholders inside and outside of the Amazon Creators team.

jobs.title: Principal Product Mgr-Tech, Creators

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of program or project management experience
- Experience using data and metrics to determine and drive improvements
- Experience owning program strategy, end to end delivery, and communicating results to senior leadership
jobs.description:
Are you passionate about improving the quality of business operations? Do you thrive in a fast-paced environment in which you have the opportunity to influence change? Would you like to enable change that drives operational efficiency and optimizes organizational productivity?

Amazon is looking for a Program Manager with a strong delivery record and proven program management experience to own strategic, tactical, cross-functional programs across the Data & AI GTM team.

This role requires scoping and creating project plans, developing processes, coordinating and driving execution, and communicating to senior management on status, risks and process changes. Cross-team coordination, project management and executive presentation skills are essential. The ideal candidate will leverage extensive program management experience, combined with deep analytical capabilities and a keen sense of innovation, to lead program from conception to completion.

We are actively seeking an experienced Program Manager with a strong background in strategic planning to lead key initiatives that align with our long-term business objectives. This role is ideal for individuals who excel in transforming visions into actionable plans and guiding those plans to fruition. In this role, you will be identifying critical business opportunities and challenges, and in devising innovative solutions to address them. You will work closely with various internal teams and stakeholders, harmonizing different perspectives and ensuring cohesive efforts towards common goals.

Key job responsibilities
Manage and oversee various cross-functional programs, ensuring successful delivery.
• Develop and refine program strategies, effectively leading and motivating teams.
• Set clear objectives for programs, leveraging data analytics to drive improvements and achieve metrics.
• Navigate and resolve ambiguities in business scenarios, applying creative problem-solving skills.
• Foster collaboration to optimize resource allocation and project success.
• Bridge gaps in team dynamics, processes, and systems, preventing common program pitfalls.
• Identify and mitigate risks, preventing them from escalating into major issues.
• Regularly communicate with senior managers, business leaders, and other stakeholders to support key business initiatives.
• Develop, implement, and oversee KPI reporting for a program portfolio, ensuring clear visibility of milestones and project performance.

About the team
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.
Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.
Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.
Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.
Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

jobs.description:
GAR (Global Accts Rec) is looking for a proactive, customer and detail oriented Collections Analyst whose main responsibility will be to manage and resolve receivables related queries received from Amazon customers. As a Collections Analyst, you will support Amazon’s customers to resolve billing issues, reconcile accounts and drive monthly collection targets. The successful individual is expected to be self-motivated, be a quick learner, have strong ownership and earn trust with our customers to facilitate timely payment



Key job responsibilities
Key job responsibilities
• Understanding of Accounts Receivables process
• Follow-up with customers via emails and phone calls to ensure timely collection of payments for assigned portfolio
• Maintain detailed post correspondence update on accounts receivable records to ensure aging is up to date, credits and collections are applied, uncollectible amounts are accounted for, and miscellaneous differences are cleared
• Collaborate with various stakeholders (Billing, Finance, Legal, Tax, Customer Service) to resolve customer disputes and queries, to establish and maintain good client relationships, both internally and externally
• Monitor high-risk accounts and ensure timely escalation of challenges to management
• Continually look at ways to improve the customer experience
• Assist in streamlining and improving the accounts receivable process by identifying areas of improvement
• Meet the monthly productivity goals
• Reconcile complex accounts and have excellent attention to detail
• Work on manual email assignment queues and have them allocated to appropriate account
jobs.description_short: GAR (Global Accts Rec) is looking for a proactive, customer and detail oriented Collections Analyst whose main responsibility will be to manage and resolve receivables related queries received from Amazon

jobs.title: FinOps Analyst AR AWS

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Experience with AI/ML technologies
- • 12+ years of professional experience with 7+ years in program management, organizational transformation, or change management roles
- • Proven track record leading org-wide initiatives across multiple functions or business units that delivered measurable business impact
- • Demonstrated success managing complex stakeholder relationships across levels and influencing without direct authority
- • Strong proficiency with data analysis, business case development, and ROI modeling
- • Background in finance operations, shared services, or similar operational environments preferred
jobs.description:
Finance Operations is seeking a strategic L6 Program Manager to lead AI Enablement across FinOps Support Centers (SCs). This role will design and implement frameworks that democratize AI capabilities, enabling teams to leverage AI to drive operational excellence through intelligent automation. This role requires a strategic leader who can balance executive stakeholder management with cross-functional coordination, change management expertise with technical fluency, and strategic thinking with pragmatic execution.

You will build the capability development programs, enablement frameworks, and partnership mechanisms that transform FinOps SC employees into AI-capable problem solvers who can build self-help tools and prototypes using their business knowledge. This includes creating structured learning pathways that enable employees to graduate from foundational AI skills to active problem-solving, facilitating partnerships with Tech teams to scale prototypes into production solutions, and measuring success by how many employees are actively solving business problems with AI—not just completing training.


Key job responsibilities
Strategy & Execution
• Translate the AI Enablement vision into executable roadmaps, aligning initiatives with FinOps goals and strategic priorities across all functions
Capability Development & AI Builder Enablement
• Design and execute programs that transform FinOps SC employees into AI-capable problem solvers who can build self-help tools and prototypes using their business knowledge
• Build scalable playbooks, role-based pathways (AI Explorer → Practitioner → Champion), and 'office hours' to support employees at varying levels of AI proficiency
• Drive adoption of AI tools through internal communications, workshops, and an AI Champions network to foster a culture of AI-first thinking and active problem-solving
Governance & Partnerships
• Collaborate with Legal, Security, and FORGE teams to operationalize Responsible AI standards and ensure all tools meet security and data privacy requirements
• Partner with Tech teams, and other stakeholders to facilitate partnerships, scale prototypes into production solutions, and integrate AI into existing workflows
Use Case Development & Scaling
• Enable employees to identify high-impact use cases, build proof-of-concepts, and articulate business value to Tech teams for scaling
• Create frameworks that help program graduates partner effectively with FinAuto and FinTech to scale employee-built prototypes into production solutions
Measurement & Impact
• Define and track KPIs for AI adoption (training completion, active tool usage, prototypes developed, solutions scaled), productivity gains, and ROI to provide transparency to senior leadership
• Conduct impact assessments that quantify business outcomes from employee-built AI solutions, including productivity gains, quality improvements, and cost savings
Continuous Improvement & Community
• Facilitate peer learning through AI Champions showcases, monthly sessions, AMAs, and community forums that accelerate adoption and innovation
• Establish mechanisms that capture learnings from frontline teams and inform continuous program improvement, ensuring enablement remains relevant and effective
